target: data/sections.json §4.2.2 (id=p4-422) — two micro-edits to address ISS-1690 (3.6× phrasing ambiguity) and ISS-1691 (residual 'strawman' polemical adjective). Both are moderate-severity copy-edits flagged in EXP-253 cycle-1 curmudgeon review.

patches.target_paragraph_anchor: The dome must either accept its own falsification criterion (...trilemma closing paragraph)
patches.before: since the BOU 2017 baseline of −10.9 nT sits approximately 10.8 nT above the ±3 nT ceiling (i.e., roughly 3.6× the threshold: 10.9 / 3 ≈ 3.6), well outside the dome’s own ±3 nT acceptance window
patches.after: since the BOU 2017 baseline of −10.9 nT is roughly 3.6× the rule’s ±3 nT half-width (|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6), well outside the dome’s own acceptance window
patches.rationale: EXP-253 cycle-1 curmudgeon review hole #1: '3.6× outside the ±3 nT envelope' is ambiguous (could read as 3.6× the magnitude of the half-width, or 3.6 envelope-widths past the edge — different numbers). The integrated text further muddied this by introducing a spurious '10.8 nT above the ±3 nT ceiling' figure (|−10.9| − 3 = 7.9, not 10.8) inside a doubly nested parenthetical. Replacement uses the curmudgeon's recommended explicit-arithmetic phrasing — names the metric ('the rule's ±3 nT half-width'), shows the division (|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6), and drops the spurious intermediate figure. No quantitative claim is changed; the |−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6 arithmetic is unchanged from the integrated text.

patches.target_paragraph_anchor: Acknowledging Chapman's Sq mechanism in a footnote and then writing the falsification rule as if Chapman doesn't exist...
patches.before: leaves the published test discriminating only against the strawman 0 nT, not against the 5–25 nT
patches.after: leaves the published test discriminating only against zero, not against the 5–25 nT
patches.rationale: EXP-253 cycle-1 curmudgeon review hole #2 (lead paragraph 'replaced with a strawman') was partially fixed during the decider's EXP-253 integration — the lead paragraph now reads 'treated as zero in the dome's published rule' (verified zero matches for 'replaced with a strawman' in part4.html at this run). What remains is one adjectival use, 'the strawman 0 nT', in the body paragraph that names the operational gap between Chapman's mechanism and the falsification rule. 'Strawman' as an adjective still imputes deliberate misframing, while the issue asks for a factual description of the threshold mismatch. Dropping 'the strawman' to leave 'zero' yields a strictly factual contrast (test fires against zero, not against 5–25 nT) without changing the argument or any cited fact.

holes_addressed: ISS-1690: closing-paragraph 3.6× phrasing ambiguity (curmudgeon hole #1, moderate)
ISS-1691: residual 'strawman 0 nT' polemical adjective in body paragraph (curmudgeon hole #2, moderate — lead-paragraph half already absorbed during EXP-253 integration)

anticipated_objections.objection: Why drop the '10.8 nT above the ±3 nT ceiling' figure? Doesn't it strengthen the argument by stacking two metrics?
anticipated_objections.response: The figure is arithmetically wrong (|−10.9| − 3 = 7.9, not 10.8) AND it competes with the 3.6× ratio for reader attention. A defender skimming the parenthetical sees the arithmetic mismatch and gets a freebie distraction. Dropping it leaves the cleaner 3.6× claim with explicit derivation, which is what the curmudgeon recommended.

anticipated_objections.objection: Removing 'strawman' in the body paragraph weakens the rhetorical punch.
anticipated_objections.response: The structural argument — that the published rule tests against zero while Chapman predicts 5–25 nT — is fully present without the adjective. Calling 0 nT a 'strawman' adds a value judgment but no new information. The reader can draw the strawman inference themselves from the contrast we lay out; doing it for them invites a 'you're imputing motive' counter that EXP-253's anticipated_objections #4 acknowledged but didn't fully close. Removing the adjective forecloses that counter at zero rhetorical cost.

anticipated_objections.objection: ISS-1691's named string ('replaced with a strawman') is no longer in the section. Why is this EXP touching anything?
anticipated_objections.response: The decider's EXP-253 integration applied a partial soft-fix to the lead paragraph but left the body paragraph adjective in place AND left ISS-1691 open. The issue scope is the polemical 'strawman' framing in §4.2.2; the lead instance is already gone, so what remains in scope is the body adjective. The proposal-ISS-1691-resolution.json file accompanying this EXP documents the partial-pre-fix and recommends close-on-integration.

anticipated_objections.objection: These edits don't add new content — they just trim. Is an EXP the right vehicle?
anticipated_objections.response: Per the parent curmudgeon review's recommended_action ('minor_edit') and the decider's EXP-278 yeet routing, yes — the priority-queue tracker and expansion-tracker both classify low-priority editorial fixes as expansions. The alternative is direct decider self-patches, which the decider has historically reserved for typo-class fixes; substantive copy-edits to load-bearing prose go through analyst review for kernel-of-truth and argument-preservation checks.

kernel_of_truth.description: The closing paragraph and body paragraph were both genuine attempts to make the §4.2.2 trilemma legible — the parenthetical arithmetic was meant to give the reader a concrete '×3' anchor for how far BOU 2017 is from the dome's own falsification window, and the 'strawman' adjective was meant to flag that 0 nT isn't a neutral physical baseline but a chosen comparator. Both intuitions are correct; both are preserved in the cleaner replacement language. The 3.6× ratio survives unchanged; the contrast between the rule's 0 nT comparator and Chapman's 5–25 nT survives unchanged.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_the_dome: These edits are within-side cleanup, not concession. The structural argument — dome's rule tests against the wrong comparator, dome's own input baseline already falls outside that wrong comparator's window — is identical pre- and post-patch. No verdict changes; no cited number changes.

tldr_review.rationale: TLDR contains neither the '3.6×' phrasing nor the 'strawman' adjective — it stays factual ('treats globe predicts 0 nT as the test', 'sits outside its ±3 nT globe window'). No TLDR edit needed.

notes_for_decider: Apply both patches as ordinary substring find-and-replace on data/sections.json. After patching: (1) re-validate the JSON, (2) close ISS-1690 and ISS-1691 (resolution proposals at monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1690-resolution.json and proposal-ISS-1691-resolution.json), (3) mark this EXP integrated in expansion-tracker.json. No build-data changes — this is prose-only.
